## Use Code

The `useCode` hook provides programmatic access to code display, editing, and transformation functionality within `CodeHighlighter` components. It's designed for scenarios where you need fine-grained control over code behavior or want to build custom code interfaces that focus purely on code management, without component rendering.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Architecture
  - Basic Usage
  - Types
  - Advanced Usage
    - URL Management and File Navigation
    - Controlling URL Hash Behavior
      - fileHashMode
      - saveHashVariantToLocalStorage
      - Examples

    - Accessing Transform Functionality

  - URL Hash Patterns
    - Default Variant

    - useSourceEditing

  - Source Enhancers
    - Enhancer Basic Usage
    - Passing State to Enhancers
    - Async Enhancers
    - Chaining Enhancers
    - Using Comments
    - Build-Time, Server-Time, and Client-Time Enhancement
    - Enhancers vs Transformers
    - Important Considerations
  - Best Practices
    - 1\. Leverage Automatic URL Generation
    - 2\. Handle Deep-Linking and URL States
    - 3\. Focus on Code Management
    - 4\. Handle Loading States
    - 5\. Leverage Options for Initial State
  - Performance Considerations

- Exports:

    - Parameters: contentProps, opts

- Types: CodeComponentsContext, UseCodeOpts, UseCodeResult

</details>

## Use Demo

The `useDemo` hook extends `useCode` functionality to provide a complete demo rendering solution that combines component previews with code display. It's specifically designed for creating interactive demonstrations where users can see both working React components and their source code.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Key Features
  - Basic Usage
  - Advanced Usage
    - Full Interactive Demo Interface
    - Editable Demo (Live Editing)
  - Types
    - `useDemo`
    - `createStackBlitz`
    - `createCodeSandbox`
    - `flattenCodeVariant`
    - `exportVariant`
  - Additional Types
  - URL Management and Deep-Linking
    - Automatic URL Hash Generation
    - Deep-Linking to Specific Files
    - URL Management Behavior
  - Integration Patterns
    - Standard Usage Pattern
    - Demo Factory Integration
  - Best Practices
    - 1\. Always Pass Props Directly
    - 2\. Conditional UI Elements
    - 3\. Simple Transform Toggle
    - 4\. Leverage URL-Based Demo Properties
  - Common Patterns
    - Simple Demo Display
    - Demo with File Navigation
    - Demo with Language Toggle
    - Reset Focus After Interaction
  - Performance Considerations
  - Error Handling
  - Troubleshooting
    - Component Not Rendering
    - Code Not Showing
    - Name/Slug Not Generated
    - URL Hash Issues
  - Related
- Exports:
  - addHiddenInput
    - Parameters: form, name, value
  - createCodeSandbox
    - Parameters: (flattenedFiles, rootFile)
  - createStackBlitz
    - Parameters: (description, flattenedFiles, rootFile, title)
  - defaultHtmlTemplate
    - Parameters: (description, entrypoint, head, language, title)
  - exportVariant
    - Parameters: variantCode, config
  - exportVariantAsCra
    - Parameters: variantCode, config
  - flattenCodeVariant
    - Parameters: variant
  - generateEntrypointFilename
    - Parameters: existingFiles, sourceFilename, useTypescript, pathPrefix
  - getFilenameFromVariant
    - Parameters: variantCode
  - getRelativeImportPath
    - Parameters: sourceFilename
  - openWithForm
    - Parameters: (formData, method, target, url)
  - useDemo
    - Parameters: contentProps, opts
- Types: ExportConfig, FlatFile, FlattenedFiles

</details>

## Use Search

The `useSearch` hook provides a powerful client-side search engine using [Orama](https://github.com/oramasearch/orama) for documentation sites. It handles index creation, search queries, and result formatting with built-in support for stemming, grouping, faceting, and customizable result types.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Basic Usage
  - Configuration
    - Stemming and Stop Words
    - Fuzzy Matching
    - Result Limiting
    - Including Private Pages
    - Category Grouping
    - Field Boosting
  - Custom Processing
    - Custom Flattening
    - Custom Formatting
  - URL Building
  - Performance
  - Creating the Sitemap
    - 1\. Extract Page Metadata
    - 2\. Define the Sitemap
    - 3\. Import in useSearch
  - Best Practices
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useSearch
    - Parameters: options
- Types: defaultSearchBoost

</details>

## Use Types

## Use Type

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
    - Key Features
  - Usage

## Use Code Window

The `useCodeWindow` hook layers code-block-specific choreography on top of [`useScrollAnchor`](../use-scroll-anchor/page.mdx) so a syntax-highlighted snippet can expand and collapse without the user losing their place — and without the horizontal scrollbar snapping in or out.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - What it does
  - Basic Usage
  - Configuration
    - `expandDuration` / `collapseDuration`
    - `scrollBackDuration`
    - `anchorSelector`
    - `collapsibleProbeSelector`
  - CSS contract
  - Examples
    - Collapsible Code Block
    - Collapsible Demo
    - `@focus` with Multiple Regions
    - Indent Shifting
    - Focus Max Size
  - API Reference
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useCodeWindow
    - Parameters: options
- Types: UseCodeWindowOptions, UseCodeWindowResult

</details>

## Use Preference

The `usePreference` hook provides specialized preference management for code demo variants and transformations. It builds on `useLocalStorageState` with intelligent storage key generation, prefix support, and automatic optimization for single-option scenarios.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Variant Preferences
    - Transform Preferences
  - Advanced Usage
    - With Custom Prefix
    - Multiple Preferences
  - Types
    - `usePreference`
    - `usePreferences`
  - Additional Types
  - How It Works
    - Intelligent Optimization
    - Demo Configuration Panel
  - Implementation Details
    - Storage Key Format
    - Storage Key Algorithm
    - Prefix System
  - Related
- Exports:
  - usePreference
    - Parameters: type, name, initializer
  - usePreferences
- Types: PreferencesContext

</details>

## Use URL Hash State

The `useUrlHashState` hook provides a simple way to synchronize component state with the URL hash fragment, enabling deep linking, state persistence, and browser navigation support in documentation and demo pages.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Common Patterns
    - Tab Navigation
    - Complex State Persistence
    - Auto-scrolling to Sections
    - Modal Deep Linking
  - Advanced Usage
    - History Management
    - Demo State Persistence
    - Hierarchical Navigation
  - Types
  - How It Works
  - When to Use
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useUrlHashState

</details>

## Use Local-Storage State

The `useLocalStorageState` hook provides persistent state management using localStorage with cross-tab synchronization, server-side rendering support, and a useState-like API. It's designed for user preferences, demo state, and any data that should persist across browser sessions.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Usage Examples
    - Basic Persistence
    - Function Updates
    - Conditional Persistence
    - Demo Code Editor
    - User Preferences Panel
  - How It Works
    - Server-Side Rendering
    - Cross-Tab Synchronization
    - Value Management
    - Null Key Behavior
  - Error Handling
  - Types
  - Performance Considerations
  - Browser Support
  - When to Use
  - Limitations
  - Related
- Exports:
  - default
    - Parameters: key, initializer

</details>

## Use Scroll Anchor

The `useScrollAnchor` hook keeps an anchor element visually fixed in the viewport while a nearby container element changes size. It is most useful when content is being inserted or expanded **above** the element the user is focused on — that's the case where they would otherwise lose their place.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Why?
  - Why not browser scroll anchoring?
  - See it in action
    - Comparing browser anchoring with the hook
    - Safari has no native anchoring at all
  - Basic Usage
  - Anchoring inside a scroll container
  - How it works
  - When to use it
  - API Reference
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useScrollAnchor
- Types: UseScrollAnchorResult

</details>

## Use Copier

The `useCopier` hook provides robust clipboard copy functionality with success state management, error handling, and customizable callbacks. It's designed for code blocks, buttons, and interactive elements that need copy-to-clipboard functionality.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Basic Usage
  - Advanced Usage
    - Dynamic Content
    - With Error Handling
    - With Custom Analytics
  - Types
  - How It Works
  - State Management
  - Error Handling
  - When to Use
  - Browser Support
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useCopier
    - Parameters: contents, opts
- Types: UseCopierOpts

</details>

## Use Errors

The `useErrors` hook provides access to error state in an isomorphic error handling system. It implements the [Props Context Layering pattern](../../patterns/props-context-layering/page.mdx) to work seamlessly across server and client boundaries, making it ideal for code highlighting and interactive demos that need robust error handling.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Types
  - Usage Examples
    - Basic Error Display
    - Isomorphic Error Handler
    - Conditional Rendering Based on Errors
    - Custom Error Formatting
  - How It Works
    - Props Context Layering Pattern
    - Isomorphic Design
    - The Problem This Solves
    - Architectural Pattern
    - Implementation Pattern
    - Multi-Layer Error Flow
    - Why This Pattern Is Essential
    - Context Integration
    - Error Flow
  - Integration with CodeHighlighter
  - Error Context Type
  - Best Practices
    - 1\. Always Check for Errors
    - 2\. Provide Meaningful Fallbacks
    - 3\. Use in Error Boundaries
  - When to Use
  - Related
- Exports:
  - useErrors
    - Parameters: props

</details>
